title: AVE Record
description: Agentic Vulnerability Enumeration — static definition of one behavioral vulnerability class for agentic AI components. Schema v1.0.0.

ave_id.description: Unique identifier. Format: AVE-YYYY-NNNNN. Immutable once published. Wrong or obsolete records are deprecated, never renumbered or deleted.

schema_version.description: AVE schema version this record was authored against, e.g. 1.0.0.

status.description: Lifecycle status. active: published and current. deprecated: superseded or withdrawn — record stays for reference. draft: not yet peer-reviewed.

published.description: ISO 8601 datetime of first publication, e.g. 2026-04-01T09:00:00Z.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 datetime of most recent update. Optional.

attack_class.description: Behavioral category, e.g. external_instruction_fetch, tool_description_injection, rug_pull. Use snake_case. Not a vulnerability_type string.

component_type.description: Kind of agent component primarily affected. Optional — omit if the class spans multiple types.

description.description: Full narrative description. Explain the mechanism, why conventional tools miss it, and the worst-case impact.

behavioral_fingerprint.description: One or two sentences describing what the component DOES that is dangerous. Behavioral, not a byte signature. A second implementer should be able to write a detection rule from this alone.

behavioral_vector.description: Short tags summarising the attack path. Optional. e.g. supply-chain, external-fetch, self-modification.

severity.description: Severity. Must agree with aivss.aivss_score. CRITICAL requires >= 9.0. HIGH: 7.0-8.9. MEDIUM: 4.0-6.9. LOW: < 4.0.

aivss.description: OWASP AIVSS v0.8 full scoring breakdown.

aarf.description: Agentic Amplification and Risk Factors — 10 sub-scores 0.0-1.0. Optional.

tool_use.description: Access to external tools or capabilities.

self_modification.description: Ability to alter own instructions at runtime.

dynamic_identity.description: Ability to assume different identities or personas.

persistent_memory.description: Access to persistent storage across sessions.

natural_language_input.description: Degree to which natural language drives behavior.

external_dependencies.description: Reliance on external URLs, APIs, or remote content.

aars.description: Agentic Amplification and Reachability Score (0-10). Derived from AARF factors.

thm.description: Threat and Heuristic Multiplier (0.5-1.5). 1.0 = neutral. Reflects exploit availability and in-the-wild evidence.

mitigation_factor.description: Mitigation factor (0-1). 1.0 = no known effective mitigation.

aivss_score.description: Final composed OWASP AIVSS score (0-10). Must agree with top-level severity.

aivss_severity.description: Severity label derived from aivss_score. Optional.

spec_version.description: OWASP AIVSS specification version. Always 0.8 for this schema.

owasp_mcp_mapping.description: OWASP MCP Top 10 cross-reference inside the AIVSS object. Optional.

notes.description: Free-text notes on scoring rationale. Optional.

owasp_mcp.description: OWASP MCP Top 10 categories. Format: MCPNN. REQUIRED — at least one. Provides the core OWASP grounding every record must have.

owasp_mapping.description: OWASP Agentic AI Top 10 mappings. Format: ASINN. Optional — add when the class maps to the Agentic Top 10. Omit rather than force a poor fit.

mitre_atlas_mapping.description: MITRE ATLAS technique IDs. Format: AML.Txxxx or AML.Txxxx.000. Optional — add when a technique applies. Do not force a mapping; omit if no current ATLAS technique covers this class.

nist_ai_rmf_mapping.description: NIST AI RMF function and category mappings, e.g. MAP-1.5, MEASURE-2.5. Optional.

affected_platforms.description: Agent platforms known to be affected, e.g. claude-code, cursor, windsurf. Optional — fill as evidence accumulates. Do not speculate.

affected_registries.description: Skill or tool registries where this class has been observed, e.g. smithery.ai, clawhub.io. Optional.

mutation_count.description: Number of distinct real-world textual mutations observed in the wild. Optional.

indicators_of_compromise.description: Observable IOC strings. REQUIRED — at least one. These are what defenders search for. Each entry is a specific observable: a phrase pattern, a behavioral indicator, or a network signal.

detection_methodology.description: Step-by-step detection approach: static scan, semantic analysis, behavioral sandbox, network monitoring. Optional.

remediation.description: How to mitigate or prevent this class. REQUIRED — must be actionable.

kill_switch_active.description: Whether a registry-level kill switch is currently active in the Bawbel registry. Optional — defaults to false.

researcher.description: Name of the researcher or team who authored this record. REQUIRED — records must be attributable. Use 'Bawbel Security Research Team' for internally authored records.

researcher_url.description: URL for the researcher or team. Optional.

references.description: Primary sources: CVEs, papers, disclosures, scan reports. REQUIRED — at least one citable source. This is the provenance signal a skeptic checks first.

oneOf.description: Plain URI to a primary source.
Structured reference with optional tag and URL.

tag.description: Short label, e.g. CVE, Research, Disclosure, Scan.

evidence_kind_default.description: Scanner hint — default evidence_kind stamped on findings. Optional. Scanner may override per detection.

detection_stage.description: Scanner hint — earliest lifecycle stage this class is reliably detectable. Optional. static_detection: pre-scan or CI suffices. runtime_observed: live agent session required.

detection_layer.description: Scanner hint — where in the ecosystem this class surfaces. Optional. content: inside the skill/tool file. server_card: MCP server manifest. registry_metadata: registry listing. runtime: during agent execution. transport: network layer.

confidence_baseline.description: Scanner hint — base confidence for a single-engine match before FP adjustment. Optional. High-signal (e.g. hardcoded credential): 0.85-0.95. Low-signal (vague phrase): 0.40-0.55.

evidence_basis_engines.description: Scanner hint — engines capable of detecting this class. Optional. Used to populate evidence_basis on findings.

derivable_into.description: Scanner hint — toxic-flow chain IDs this class can participate in, e.g. credential-exfiltration, rug-pull-chain. Optional.
